rationale: The cited 2001 JMR study directly models satisfaction effects on share of category requirements (SCR), while the citing 2004 JMR paper’s core chain includes satisfaction  share of wallet  margin; this is a strong construct-level and mechanism overlap. The citing note also says it extends prior evidence from consumer settings, and the cited paper is exactly a consumer manufacturer-contact setting with downstream SCR behavior, so this citation is plausibly foundational/comparative rather than an obvious incidental mention.
